lesson.title: Lesson 21: William S. Hart and the Early Western Film

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Lesson 21.

translation.text: 第21课。

segments.text: S.

translation.text: 威廉·S·哈特

segments.text: and the Early Western Film.

translation.text: 与早期西部电影。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: How did William Hart's childhood prepare him for his acting role in Western films?

translation.text: 威廉·哈特童年的哪些经历为他在西部片中扮演角色做好了准备？

segments.text: William S.

translation.text: 威廉·S·哈特

segments.text: was, perhaps, the greatest of all Western stars.

translation.text: 也许是所有西部片明星中最伟大的一位。

segments.text: For unlike Gary Cooper and John Wayne, he appeared in nothing but Westerns.

translation.text: 因为与加里·库珀和约翰·韦恩不同，他只出演西部片。

segments.text: From 1914 to 1924, he was supreme and unchallenged.

translation.text: 从1914年到1924年，他独占鳌头，无人能敌。

segments.text: It was Hart who created the basic formula of the Western film, and devised the protagonist he played in every film he made.

translation.text: 正是哈特创造了西部片的基本模式，也设计出他在每部影片中扮演的主人公。

segments.text: The good bad man, the accidental noble outlaw, or the honest but framed cowboy, or the sheriff made suspect by vicious gossip.

translation.text: 善良的坏人、偶然走上不法道路的高尚歹徒、诚实却遭陷害的牛仔，或因恶意流言而受到怀疑的警长。

segments.text: In short, the individual in conflict with himself and his frontier environment.

translation.text: 简而言之，是一个与自身及边疆环境发生冲突的人。

segments.text: Unlike most of his contemporaries in Hollywood, Hart actually knew something of the Old West.

translation.text: 与好莱坞大多数同代人不同，哈特确实了解一些昔日西部的情况。

segments.text: He had lived in it as a child when it was already disappearing, and his hero was firmly rooted in his memories and experiences, and in both the history and the mythology of the vanished frontier.

translation.text: 童年时，他曾生活在那片已经消逝的西部；他的英雄形象深深植根于自身记忆和经历，也植根于那片消失边疆的历史与神话。

segments.text: And although no period or place in American history has been more absurdly romanticized, myth and reality did join hands in at least one arena.

translation.text: 虽然美国历史上没有任何时期或地方被如此荒谬地浪漫化，但至少在一个领域，神话与现实确实携手相遇了。

segments.text: The conflict between the individual and encroaching civilization.

translation.text: 那就是个人与不断逼近的文明之间的冲突。

segments.text: Men accustomed to struggling for survival against the elements and Indians were bewildered by politicians, bankers and businessmen, and unhorsed by fences, laws, and alien taboos.

translation.text: 那些习惯同自然和印第安人抗争求生的人，被政客、银行家和商人弄得困惑不解，又被篱笆、法律和陌生禁忌赶下了马。

segments.text: Hart's good bad man was always an outsider, always one of the disinherited, and if he found it necessary to shoot a sheriff or rob a bank along the way, his early audiences found it easy

translation.text: 哈特笔下善良的坏人永远是局外人、永远属于被剥夺者；即使途中不得不开枪打警长或抢银行，他早期的观众也很容易

segments.text: to understand and forgive, especially when it was Hart who, in the end, the attacking Indians.

translation.text: 理解并原谅他，尤其因为最终总是哈特智胜来犯的印第安人。

segments.text: Audiences in the second decade of the twentieth century found it pleasant to escape to a time when life, though hard, was relatively simple.

translation.text: 二十世纪第二个十年的观众乐于逃回一个生活虽艰苦、却相对简单的时代。

segments.text: We still do.

translation.text: 我们今天依然如此。

segments.text: Living in a world in which undeclared aggression, war, hypocrisy, chicanery, anarchy, and impending immolation are part of our daily lives, we all want a code to live by.

translation.text: 生活在一个不宣而战的侵略、战争、虚伪、欺诈、无政府状态和迫在眉睫的毁灭已成为日常的世界里，我们都渴望一套生活准则。
